lede: A look at January 2 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Lynn Conway and Rudolf Clausius, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Stardust (spacecraft)
events.desc: Stardust successfully flies past Comet Wild 2, collecting samples that are returned to Earth.

events.title: Sri Lankan civil war
events.desc: Sri Lankan Civil War: The Sri Lanka Navy kill 35–100 civilians on the Jaffna Lagoon.

events.title: Sharon Pratt
events.desc: Sharon Pratt Dixon becomes the first African American woman mayor of a major city and first woman Mayor of the District of Columbia.

events.title: Gale of January 1976
events.desc: The Gale of January 1976 begins, resulting in coastal flooding around the southern North Sea coasts, affecting countries from Ireland to Yugoslavia and causing at least 82 deaths and US$1.3 billion in damage.

events.title: Ronald Reagan
events.desc: Ronald Reagan, past movie actor and future President of the United States, is sworn in as Governor of California.

events.title: Vietnam War
events.desc: Vietnam War: The Viet Cong wins its first major victory, at the Battle of Ap Bac.

events.title: Luna 1
events.desc: Luna 1, the first spacecraft to reach the vicinity of the Moon and to orbit the Sun, is launched by the Soviet Union.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Cardiff Blitz severely damages the cathedral in Cardiff, Wales.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: American forces under the command of General George Washington repulse a British attack led by General Charles Cornwallis at the Battle of the Assunpink Creek near Trenton, New Jersey.

events.title: Pope John II
events.desc: Mercurius becomes Pope John II, the first pope to adopt a new name upon elevation to the papacy.

births.name: Lynn Conway
births.line: American computer scientist and electrical engineer

births.name: Saud bin Faisal Al Saud
births.line: Saudi Arabian economist and politician, Saudi Arabian Minister of Foreign Affairs

deaths.text: George Biddell Airy, English mathematician and astronomer
Heinrich Reuß von Plauen, Grand Master of the Teutonic Order

observances.text: Christian feast day: Basil the Great (Catholic Church and Church of England)
Christian feast day: Gregory of Nazianzus (Catholic Church)
Christian feast day: Blessed Marie Anne Blondin
Christian feast day: Seraphim of Sarov (Eastern Orthodox Church)
